00_overview.section_description: Executive summary of India-Pakistan bilateral relationship

executive_summary.one_liner: Nuclear-armed neighbors with shared history, divided by partition, disputed territory, and 77+ years of conflict

relationship_in_numbers.source: Ministry of Defence India, ISPR Pakistan

key_characteristics.description: Relationship fundamentally shaped by 1947 British India partition
key_characteristics.impact: Ongoing disputes over Kashmir, refugee trauma, communal tensions

key_characteristics.description: Both nations declared nuclear powers since 1998
key_characteristics.impact: MAD doctrine prevents full-scale war but enables sub-conventional conflict

status.india_position: Kashmir is integral part of India; Article 370 abrogation (2019) is internal matter
status.pakistan_position: Kashmir is disputed territory; UN resolutions mandate plebiscite
status.source: MEA India, MOFA Pakistan official statements

status.india_position: Pakistan state sponsors terrorism; Mumbai 2008, Pulwama 2019 perpetrators sheltered in Pakistan
status.pakistan_position: India conducts false flag operations; supports insurgency in Balochistan

key_characteristics.description: Indus Waters Treaty (1960) governs water sharing
key_characteristics.status: Treaty under strain; India reviewing terms post-Pulwama 2019

last_official_visit.india_to_pakistan: December 2015 - PM Modi surprise Lahore visit

recent_developments.significance: PML-N led government; no immediate India policy change

recent_developments.event: New Parliament inauguration India - No Pakistan invite

turning_points: 2016: Pathankot, Uri attacks → Surgical strikes → Dialogue suspension
relationship_trajectory.forecast_2025_26: Unlikely thaw without major geopolitical shift

description: India position: Pakistan-backed tribal invasion of Kashmir
Pakistan position: Indigenous uprising supported by volunteers

description: India position: Humanitarian intervention to stop genocide
Pakistan position: Indian interference in internal affairs

PM Vajpayee's bus diplomacy; nuclear confidence building

description: India position: Pakistani Army/militants infiltrated Indian territory
Pakistan position: Indigenous Kashmiri militants; limited support

8-basket dialogue including Kashmir, terrorism, trade

Modi visits Sharif's home; attends granddaughter wedding

Pakistan: Illegal annexation; violation of UN resolutions

03_agreements_treaties.section_description: All bilateral agreements, treaties, and MOUs

summary_stats.source: MEA India Treaty Database; MOFA Pakistan

major_treaties.name: Indus Waters Treaty
summary: Divides 6 Indus system rivers between nations
current_issues: Disputes over Kishanganga, Ratle hydropower projects
Pakistan filed case at International Court of Arbitration (2016)
major_treaties.source: World Bank; Ministry of Jal Shakti India

major_treaties.name: Simla Agreement
summary: Both commit to bilateral resolution of disputes
Ceasefire line renamed Line of Control (LoC)

interpretations.india: Commits both to bilateralism; negates UN resolutions
interpretations.pakistan: Does not supersede UN resolutions on Kashmir

major_treaties.name: Lahore Declaration
summary: Commitment to reduce risk of nuclear war
Agreement on advance notification of missile tests
Memorandum of Understanding on nuclear CBMs attached
major_treaties.note: Undermined by Kargil War 3 months later

major_treaties.name: 2003 Ceasefire Agreement
summary: Ceasefire along LoC, International Border, Siachen

major_treaties.name: Kartarpur Corridor Agreement
summary: Visa-free access for Indian Sikhs to Gurdwara Darbar Sahib
4.5 km corridor from India to Pakistan
Service fee: $20 (waived by Pakistan on special occasions)

suspended_agreements.name: MFN Status (Trade)
suspended_agreements.pakistan_response: Pakistan never reciprocated; revoked limited MFN 2019

suspended_agreements.name: SAARC Visa Exemption Scheme
suspended_agreements.details: Dignitaries visa-free travel suspended with SAARC activities

military_cbms.name: Agreement on Non-Attack on Nuclear Facilities
Agreement on Advance Notice of Military Exercises
Agreement on Prevention of Airspace Violations

04_political_relations.section_description: Political dynamics, dialogues, disputes, and positions

kashmir.name: Jammu & Kashmir Dispute

territory_claimed.india: Entire erstwhile Princely State (including Azad Kashmir, Gilgit-Baltistan)
territory_claimed.pakistan: Entire erstwhile Princely State (including Indian-administered J&K)

india.status_2019: Reorganized into 2 Union Territories (J&K and Ladakh)

pakistan.status: AJK: Autonomous region; GB: Provisional Province (2020)

india: Kashmir is integral and inalienable part of India
Instrument of Accession (1947) is legally binding
UN resolutions outdated; Simla Agreement mandates bilateralism
pakistan: Kashmir is core dispute; self-determination is Kashmiri right
UN Security Council resolutions call for plebiscite
Simla Agreement does not supersede UNSC resolutions
India must engage in dialogue; international community should intervene

current_relevance.india_view: Obsolete; conditions not met; bilateral Simla supersedes

kashmir.source: MEA India; MOFA Pakistan; UN Archives

siachen.name: Siachen Glacier Dispute
siachen.current_status: Indian Army controls since Operation Meghdoot (1984)

positions.india: Legitimate occupation; beyond NJ9842 was undefined

military_presence.india: ~6,000 troops at heights up to 22,000 ft

siachen.source: MoD India; ISPR Pakistan; Academic (Col. Amar Cheema)

sir_creek.name: Sir Creek Maritime Boundary
sir_creek.dispute: Alignment of boundary in 96 km tidal estuary

positions.pakistan: Boundary follows eastern bank (1914 British map)

sir_creek.implications: Affects EEZ rights in Arabian Sea (potential hydrocarbons)
sir_creek.negotiations: Multiple rounds; near-agreement reported but not finalized

terrorism.name: Cross-Border Terrorism
india_position: Pakistan state sponsors terrorism against India
ISI supports LeT, JeM, Hizbul Mujahideen
Mumbai 2008, Parliament 2001, Pulwama 2019 perpetrators sheltered in Pakistan
Pakistan on FATF grey list until 2022 validates concerns
pakistan_position: India sponsors terrorism in Balochistan (alleges RAW involvement)
Kulbhushan Jadhav case proves Indian espionage
Kashmir uprising is indigenous; not terrorism

limited_areas.potential: Regional cooperation platform exists if relations improve

limited_areas.engagement: Ministerial interaction only; no bilateral use of platform

limited_areas.cooperation: None bilateral; parallel positions in COP negotiations

limited_areas.note: India offered vaccines to neighbors; Pakistan excluded from direct offer

ruling_party_position.stance: Hardline; no talks until terror stops

opposition_position.stance: Open to dialogue; critical of freeze

public_opinion.post_pulwama_2019: Hardened; 87% supported military action (India Today poll)

ruling_coalition.stance: Kashmir remains core issue; dialogue from strength

military_influence.role: De facto control over India policy

opposition.stance: Was PM 2018-22; sought dialogue but Pulwama/Article 370 led to downturn

pakistan.public_opinion: Generally supportive of government's Kashmir position

usa.position: Strategic partnership with India; alliance with Pakistan
usa.on_kashmir: Bilateral issue; no mediation unless both request

china.on_kashmir: Supports Pakistan position (CPEC runs through Gilgit-Baltistan)
china.recent: Blocked India's attempts to list terrorists at UNSC (JeM chief)

oic.action: Condemns India on Article 370; India expelled from Jeddah meeting (2019)

un.position: Calls for restraint; no recent UNSC action
un.unmogip: UN Military Observer Group in India and Pakistan still exists
un.india_stance: UNMOGIP mandate has lapsed; vestigial presence only

recent_political_events.detail: EAM Jaishankar attended; no bilateral meeting with Pakistan

regulatory_environment.pakistan: Indian investment restricted; special permission needed

proposals: CASA-1000 (Central Asia-South Asia) - India not included currently

feb_27_dogfight.pakistan_action: PAF crossed LoC in retaliation; engaged Indian jets

07_people_to_people.section_description: Diaspora, cultural ties, tourism, education, visas, civil society

shared_heritage.common_history: 5,000+ years of shared civilization; unified under British India (1857-1947)

partition_impact.divided_families: 14-18 million displaced; families separated across border

visa_regime.current_status: Highly restrictive; among world's most difficult bilateral visas

visa_categories.note: Tourism effectively banned; no tourist visas issued

details: Kartarpur Corridor: Visa-free for Indian Sikhs since Nov 2019
Annual Sikh jathas to Pakistan gurdwaras (Nankana Sahib, Panja Sahib)
Hindu pilgrims to Hinglaj Mata temple, Katas Raj (rare permissions)

visa_categories.details: Pakistani patients occasionally get Indian visas for treatment (rare); Indian patients not seeking Pakistan visas

visa_categories.previous: Limited business visas issued; cumbersome process

examples: SCO Summit 2024: Indian delegation to Islamabad

indian_diaspora_in_pakistan.composition: Mostly business families; some stuck post-partition
indian_diaspora_in_pakistan.note: Most Hindus/Sikhs migrated to India during/after partition

composition: Muslims who stayed in India post-partition

third_country_diaspora_interaction.status: Indian and Pakistani diaspora communities largely separate
third_country_diaspora_interaction.exceptions: Professional networks; some social interaction in liberal environments

student_mobility.source: UGC India; HEC Pakistan data sparse

scholarship_programs.pakistan_offers: No specific programs for Indian students

wagah_border_ceremony.significance: Only regular people-to-people visual contact point

pakistani_artists_in_bollywood.previous: Fawad Khan, Mahira Khan, Atif Aslam, Rahat Fateh Ali Khan featured

literature.book_bans: Several Pakistani authors' books unavailable in India; vice versa

cricket.politics: India refuses to tour Pakistan; neutral venues demanded

peace_activists.pakistan: Small peace constituency; overshadowed by establishment

indian_media.mainstream_narrative: Pakistan as state sponsor of terrorism
indian_media.representation: Largely negative; sensationalist coverage of conflicts

prisoners.exchange: Periodic releases; humanitarian gestures on Independence Days

missing_persons.current: Families separated; unable to get information

urdu_language.status_india: Recognized language; second official in some states
urdu_language.decline_in_india: Urdu education declining; perceived as 'Muslim language'
urdu_language.shared_literature: Ghalib, Mir, Iqbal celebrated in both countries

key_divergence_votes: Russia-Ukraine war (India abstained; Pakistan condemned Russia)

bimstec.full_name: Bay of Bengal Initiative for Multi-Sectoral Technical and Economic Cooperation
bimstec.members: Bangladesh, Bhutan, India, Myanmar, Nepal, Sri Lanka, Thailand
bimstec.india_push: India promoting BIMSTEC as SAARC alternative (excludes Pakistan)
bimstec.pakistan_view: Views BIMSTEC as Indian attempt to isolate Pakistan

quad.pakistan_concern: Sees Quad as anti-China; Pakistan aligns with China
quad.india_position: Quad not military alliance; for free Indo-Pacific

unfccc_cop.convergence: Both demand developed nations fulfill commitments

10_sectoral_cooperation.section_description: Cooperation in energy, climate, science, space, health, agriculture, and other sectors

overall_status.summary: Near-zero sectoral cooperation across all domains

potential.india_surplus: India has power surplus in some seasons/regions
potential.pakistan_deficit: Pakistan faces chronic power shortages (4,000-7,000 MW deficit)

ipi_pipeline.name: Iran-Pakistan-India Pipeline
ipi_pipeline.source: Ministry of Petroleum India; MOFA Pakistan

solar.potential: Shared technology; joint projects in border regions

hydro.pakistan_position: India violating IWT; filed international arbitration
hydro.source: Ministry of Jal Shakti India; MOFA Pakistan

nuclear_energy.pakistan_china: Pakistan's nuclear plants with Chinese assistance

shared_challenges.impact: Both dependent on Indus system; glacier retreat threatens water security
shared_challenges.potential: Joint glacier monitoring; early warning systems

shared_challenges.example: Winter 2024: Both cities AQI 400+; no coordination

shared_challenges.impact: Monsoon floods affect both (2010, 2022 Pakistan floods devastating)
shared_challenges.details: Limited data sharing per IWT; real-time sharing inconsistent

shared_challenges.impact: Both face extreme heat events (40-50°C summers)

convergence: Both demand developed nations reduce emissions
Both invoke historical responsibility of Global North
unfccc_positions.divergence: None significant; but no joint climate advocacy

academic_collaboration.barriers: Visa issues; funding restrictions; political climate

india_offers_to_neighbors.pakistan_response: Declined to participate (only SAARC nation to reject)

space.potential: Disaster early warning; communication; remote sensing

covid_19.missed_opportunity: No regional cooperation despite shared crisis
covid_19.source: MEA India; Ministry of Health Pakistan

polio.status: Both among last endemic countries (Pakistan still endemic 2024)

medical_tourism.india_hospitals: Apollo, Medanta, AIIMS attracted Pakistani patients
medical_tourism.pakistan_need: Liver transplants, cardiac surgery, cancer treatment

pharmaceutical_trade.pre_2019: India exported medicines worth $500+ million to Pakistan
pharmaceutical_trade.pakistan_dependence: Many essential medicines were Indian generics
pharmaceutical_trade.impact: Higher costs; shortages for Pakistani patients

disease_surveillance.tb: Both high-burden countries; no bilateral coordination
disease_surveillance.hepatitis: Both have significant hepatitis B/C burden

food_trade.impact: Pakistan faced onion/tomato shortages (2019-2020); imported from Iran/Afghanistan

data_sharing.status: India shares flood/flow data per treaty
data_sharing.issues: Pakistan alleges insufficient data; India denies

kishanganga.pakistan_objection: Violates IWT; affects Neelum river flow
kishanganga.source: Ministry of Jal Shakti India; MOFA Pakistan

ratle.arbitration: Pakistan requested PCA arbitration (2016); India prefers World Bank neutral expert

treaty_future.pakistan_concern: Any abrogation would be existential threat
treaty_future.expert_view: Treaty likely to survive; neither benefits from collapse

2010_pakistan_floods.india_response: Offered $5 million; Pakistan initially hesitant; accepted

early_warning.potential: Joint Himalayan monitoring; earthquake early warning

stock_market.investment: Indians cannot invest in PSX; Pakistanis cannot invest in NSE/BSE

fintech.potential: UPI-like systems could connect if relations normalize

mutual_legal_assistance.status: No formal cooperation on criminal matters

cases.india_demand: Extradition from Pakistan (1993 Mumbai blasts mastermind)
cases.pakistan_response: Claims no evidence of presence in Pakistan

prisoner_repatriation.agreement: Consular access agreement exists (not always honored)
prisoner_repatriation.kulbhushan_jadhav: ICJ case; consular access dispute ongoing

11_influence_third_parties.section_description: Major powers influencing India-Pakistan relations, triangle dynamics, proxy relationships

overview.summary: India-Pakistan relations heavily influenced by great power dynamics, especially US-China rivalry
historical_patterns: Cold War: US-Pakistan vs USSR-India alignment
Post-Cold War: US pivot toward India
21st Century: China-Pakistan vs India-US growing alignment

post_cold_war.2001_2021: Pakistan: Major Non-NATO Ally for Afghanistan war; India: Strategic partnership developing

current_2024.us_india: Comprehensive Global Strategic Partnership; Quad; defense deals

key_agreements.name: Civil Nuclear Deal (123 Agreement)
key_agreements.significance: Ended India's nuclear isolation; NSG waiver

key_agreements.name: ICET (Initiative on Critical and Emerging Technology)

us_india_partnership.platforms: C-17, C-130J, Apache, Chinook, P-8I, MH-60R

aid_decline.suspended: CSF (Coalition Support Fund) ended; military aid frozen

irritants: F-16 controversy (Pakistan using against India claim)
us_pakistan_relationship.source: US State Department; Congressional Research Service

mediation_offers.trump_2019: Offered mediation on Kashmir; India refused; Pakistan welcomed
mediation_offers.india_position: No third-party mediation; bilateral resolution only

crisis_management.us_role: Clinton pressured Sharif to withdraw Pakistani forces
Rice, Mullen urged restraint; shared intelligence
Pompeo called both sides; urged de-escalation

china.role: Pakistan's primary strategic partner; India's strategic rival

china_pakistan_relationship.characterization: All-weather strategic cooperative partnership; higher than mountains, deeper than ocean, sweeter than honey

military.platforms: JF-17 Thunder, J-10C, Type 054A frigates, VT-4 tanks

economic.debt: Pakistan owes China $30+ billion (20% of external debt)

political: China blocks India's UNSC permanent seat bid
China blocked listing Masood Azhar as terrorist (2009-2019)
China supports Pakistan on Kashmir at UNSC (2019)

border_dispute.2020_galwan_clash: 20 Indian soldiers, 4+ Chinese soldiers killed (PLA admits)

china_india_relationship.trade_2023: $118 billion (India's largest trading partner despite tensions)
strategic_rivalry: Belt and Road vs India's alternatives

china_role_in_ind_pak.strategic_calculus: Pakistan as strategic asset to constrain India
china_role_in_ind_pak.two_front_dilemma: India faces potential China-Pakistan coordinated pressure
china_role_in_ind_pak.cpec_factor: Binds China to Pakistan territorial positions (Gilgit-Baltistan)
china_role_in_ind_pak.mediation: China has not offered; not neutral party

russia.role: India's traditional partner; balancing both now

defense.platforms: Su-30MKI, T-90 tanks, S-400, BrahMos, INS Vikramaditya
defense.recent_deals: S-400 ($5.4 billion despite US sanctions threat)

russia_india_relationship.energy: India major Russian oil buyer post-Ukraine war

russia_pakistan_relationship.limitations: India still priority; surface-level improvement only

russia_role_in_ind_pak.historical: Supported India in 1971 war (vetoed UNSC resolution)
russia_role_in_ind_pak.potential: Could facilitate back-channel (historically done for Afghanistan)

saudi_arabia.role: Major partner for both; unique balancing act

developments: Saudi investment pledges ($100 billion; partial progress)
Energy partnership (India 4th largest oil buyer from Saudi)

saudi_balancing.kashmir: Avoided taking strong position (contrast to OIC)
saudi_balancing.terrorism: Funded madrassas blamed for extremism; now distancing
saudi_balancing.economic: Sees India as larger economic opportunity
saudi_balancing.shift: Visible tilt toward balancing (previously clearly pro-Pakistan)

uae.role: Emerging mediator; strong ties with both

developments: UAE invited India as 'Guest of Honor' at OIC (2019)
CEPA (trade agreement 2022): Trade up 16% in year 1

reported_backchannel.source: The Hindu, Dawn, Reuters citing sources

uae_mediation.potential: UAE well-positioned for mediation; trusted by both

uk_pakistan.political_influence: Pakistani diaspora influential in UK politics

uk_role.all_party_groups: UK Parliament has Kashmir groups (both sides lobby)

afghanistan.role: Buffer state; battleground for India-Pakistan proxy competition

india_afghanistan.historical: Strong ties; India supported Northern Alliance
india_afghanistan.projects: Parliament building, Salma Dam, roads, scholarships

pakistan_afghanistan.taliban_support: Pakistan allegedly supported Taliban (India claim; widely reported)
pakistan_afghanistan.current_issues: TTP attacks from Afghan soil; border disputes; refugee tensions

triangular_dynamics.historical: India-Pakistan competed for influence in Afghanistan
triangular_dynamics.pakistan_fear: Indian encirclement; India presence on second front
triangular_dynamics.taliban_takeover_2021: India lost influence; Pakistan's strategic asset narrative (but facing blowback via TTP)

iran.role: Neighbor to Pakistan; partner for both

india_iran.relationship: Historical ties; currently strained by US sanctions

chabahar_port.purpose: Bypass Pakistan for Afghanistan/Central Asia access

india_iran.oil_trade: Suspended (US sanctions); India was major buyer

triangular.india_chabahar: Reduces India's dependence on Pakistan route
triangular.pakistan_concern: Sees India-Iran cooperation as potential second-front threat

turkey.role: Emerging Pakistan ally; growing tension with India
turkey.impact: Turkey part of emerging Pakistan-Turkey-Malaysia axis (briefly 2019-2020)

quad_impact.pakistan_view: Anti-China grouping; sees India as aggressor

bri_vs_alternatives.india_alternatives: Asia-Africa Growth Corridor (with Japan); Chabahar; I2U2

oic_dynamics.recent: Gulf states warming to India; OIC less united
oic_dynamics.example: UAE invited India to OIC over Pakistan objections (2019)

proxy_competition.afghanistan: Primary battleground (1990s-2021); India lost influence post-Taliban

balochistan.pakistan_claim: India sponsors Baloch insurgency (RAW via Afghanistan/Iran)
balochistan.evidence: Pakistan claims Kulbhushan Jadhav case proves it

kashmir.evidence: UN-designated terror groups based in Pakistan; 26/11 linkages proven

charts.title: US Alignment Shift (Cold War to Present)

12_areas_of_concern.section_description: Current disputes, friction points, risks, and areas of competition

india: Kashmir is integral part of India
Article 370 abrogation is internal constitutional matter
No third-party mediation; Simla Agreement mandates bilateralism

human_rights.india_response: Reports biased; based on unverified information
human_rights.source: UN OHCHR; MEA India; MOFA Pakistan

kashmir.escalation_risk: High; nuclear dimension makes it world's most dangerous dispute

siachen.sticking_point: Authentication of troop positions before withdrawal

positions.pakistan: Boundary follows eastern bank (1914 British map)

india_concerns.allegation: Pakistan state sponsors terrorism against India
evidence: 26/11 Mumbai attacks: Lashkar-e-Taiba operatives from Pakistan
Pulwama 2019: JeM claimed responsibility; headquartered in Pakistan
Multiple UNSC-designated terrorists reside in Pakistan

named_terrorists.name: Hafiz Saeed
named_terrorists.status: UN-designated; convicted in Pakistan (but India demands extradition)

named_terrorists.name: Masood Azhar
named_terrorists.status: UN-designated (2019; China lifted block); whereabouts unclear
named_terrorists.india_demand: Extradition; trial for Parliament attack, Pulwama

india_concerns.india_demand: Verifiable action against terror groups; not cosmetic arrests
india_concerns.source: NIA India; UN 1267 Committee; US State Department

evidence_claimed: Kulbhushan Jadhav case (Indian national; death sentence; ICJ case)
pakistan_concerns.india_response: Jadhav was businessman; kidnapped from Iran; no terror links
Categorically denies; India itself victim of terrorism

fatf_impact.outcome: Pakistan took limited action; FATF removed from grey list Oct 2022

indus_waters_treaty_strain.india_statement: Blood and water cannot flow together - PM Modi (2016)
actions_considered: Maximizing use of allocated eastern rivers
indus_waters_treaty_strain.pakistan_concern: Any IWT abrogation is existential threat (80% water from Indus system)

current_disputes.pakistan_objection: Violates IWT; reduces Neelum river flow
current_disputes.arbitration: PCA partial award (2013); some issues remain
Pakistan sought PCA arbitration; India prefers World Bank neutral expert; parallel proceedings

climate_factor.glacier_melt: Himalayan glaciers retreating; long-term water availability uncertain
climate_factor.impact: Both countries face water stress; cooperation needed but absent

water_disputes.source: Ministry of Jal Shakti India; Pakistan Indus Water Commissioner; World Bank

concerns.detail: Pakistan's Full Spectrum Deterrence includes tactical nukes
concerns.scenario: India conventional attack → Pakistan tactical nuke response → escalation

concerns.detail: Pakistan's nuclear security questioned (A.Q. Khan network history)
concerns.pakistan_response: World-class security; US has praised improvements

concerns.detail: No red phone; limited crisis communication mechanisms
concerns.example: 2019 aerial skirmish escalated rapidly; international intervention needed

concerns.detail: India has NFU; Pakistan does not; India's NFU questioned by some statements

infiltration.data: Infiltration down significantly (2021-2024) per Indian sources

dimensions.detail: Nationalist media on both sides escalates tensions
Bot networks; fake news; propaganda campaigns
Mutual allegations of hacking; website defacements
dimensions.incidents: AIIMS hack (2022; attribution unclear); Pakistani govt sites hacked

indicators: High Commissioners absent (Chargé d'affaires level only)
consequences: No official channel for crisis communication
No mechanism to discuss shared challenges

areas.competition: UNSC seat (India bid; Pakistan opposes); NSG membership; OIC influence
Both sought influence; India sidelined post-Taliban takeover
Pakistan claims leadership; India building Gulf ties
Highest stakes matches globally; no bilateral series

high_probability_high_impact: Another terror attack triggering military response

13_future_outlook.section_description: Short, medium, and long-term scenarios, wildcards, prerequisites for improvement

baseline_assessment.current_trajectory: Frozen relations; no improvement expected without major shift
baseline_assessment.momentum: Negative since 2016; stabilized at low point
baseline_assessment.key_variable: Domestic politics in both countries prioritize hardline positions

most_likely_scenario.name: Continued Stalemate
description: Relations remain frozen at current level
Occasional diplomatic tensions but no major crisis
drivers: India: BJP government maintains hardline; no political incentive to engage
Pakistan: Military maintains India policy; no civilian space for outreach
No external pressure for dialogue (US disengaged; China supports Pakistan)

alternative_scenarios.name: Minor Thaw
triggers: India seeks regional stability for development goals

alternative_scenarios.name: Escalation
description: Major terror attack on India traced to Pakistan
International intervention to prevent nuclear escalation
triggers: JeM/LeT attack on security forces or civilians
Domestic political pressures force strong response

scenarios.name: Managed Rivalry
description: Limited dialogue on specific issues (terrorism, water)

scenarios.name: Deepening Hostility
description: Water treaty strain leads to crisis
Regional instability affects global supply chains

scenarios.name: Breakthrough
prerequisites: Leadership change with peace mandate (both sides)

possible_futures.name: Status Quo Persistence
description: India becomes $10T+ economy; gap with Pakistan widens

possible_futures.name: Regional Integration
description: SAARC revived or replaced by new framework

possible_futures.name: Pakistan State Fragility
possible_futures.implications: Even this adverse scenario might force India engagement

possible_futures.name: Major Conflict
description: Full-scale war triggered by accumulating tensions
Nuclear weapons potentially used (limited or full)

positive_wildcards.impact: Could shift public opinion; create political space
Shared Himalayan challenges require joint response
Pakistan more amenable to India engagement

positive_wildcards.wildcard: US-China agreement includes South Asia stability

positive_wildcards.impact: Pragmatic elites push for trade normalization

negative_wildcards.wildcard: Major terror attack with mass casualties
Water war triggered by unilateral dam actions
negative_wildcards.impact: Existential for Pakistan; major escalation risk

negative_wildcards.wildcard: Hardline government escalates for domestic politics

india_actions_needed: Credible dialogue offer with clear agenda
Proportionate response to terrorist incidents (not maximalist)
Economic engagement separated from political issues
pakistan_actions_needed: Verifiable action against anti-India terror groups
Shift from 'bleed India' to 'engage India' strategy
Civilian control over India policy (reduce military dominance)
Acceptance of Article 370 reality as starting point
Focus on economic development over strategic parity
mutual_actions_needed: Park Kashmir; address other issues first
international_role: China: Could pressure Pakistan (unlikely given CPEC)
Gulf states: Well-positioned to mediate (UAE model)
UN: Limited role unless both invite

scenario_planning_summary.transformative_2035: Economic integration underway; peace dividends realized

14_imaginary_peace_scenario.section_description: Vision of normalized relations, quantified benefits, roadmap, inspirational possibilities

vision_statement.title: The South Asian Union: From Partition to Partnership
vision_statement.tagline: What if the two nations chose cooperation over confrontation?
vision_statement.inspiration: The European Union emerged from the ashes of two World Wars between France and Germany. India and Pakistan, sharing millennia of common heritage, could forge a similar transformation.

year_2030_vision: Open borders: Citizens cross freely with ID cards (like India-Nepal)
Economic integration: Bilateral trade exceeds $50 billion annually
Connectivity: Delhi-Lahore high-speed rail completes journey in 4 hours
Energy: Shared power grid; gas pipelines operational
Security: Joint border patrols; intelligence sharing on terrorism
Kashmir: Autonomous governance; soft borders; residents travel freely
Nuclear: Arms reduction treaty; joint safeguards
Culture: Bollywood-Lollywood collaboration; joint cricket leagues
Youth: Student exchanges; 10,000+ studying in each other's countries

transit_trade.india_central_asia: Land access via Pakistan (vs sea route)

people_to_people_gains.diaspora_benefit: UK, US, Gulf diaspora tensions reduced

regional_benefits.saarc_revival: Regional trade could reach $100 billion (vs current $35 billion)

total_economic_opportunity.note: Figures are estimates based on academic studies and projections

actions.action: Trade expanded; MFN status granted mutually
Bus services restored; new routes added
Direct flights resumed; airspace fully opened
Pakistan verifiable action on terror groups

models_considered: Autonomous regions with free movement (EU internal borders)

actions.action: SAARC revival or South Asian Union formation
Cultural renaissance (joint film production, music, literature)

kashmir_resolution_models.note: These are academic/think-tank proposals, not official positions

models.name: Soft Borders Model
elements: LoC remains but becomes soft border
Residents can live, work, travel both sides

models.name: Autonomy Plus
elements: Foreign affairs, defense with respective governments

models.name: International Guarantee
elements: UN or major power guarantee of settlement

france_germany.transformation: EU founding members; Franco-German axis drives Europe
france_germany.peace_dividend: €500+ billion in avoided military spending; shared prosperity
france_germany.lesson: Economic integration created shared stake in peace

vietnam_usa.peace_dividend: Vietnam's economic rise; US ally in Indo-Pacific
vietnam_usa.lesson: Former enemies can become close partners within decades

china_japan.current: Competing but economically intertwined; $300+ billion trade
china_japan.peace_dividend: East Asian economic miracle partly built on integration
china_japan.lesson: Even without full reconciliation, economic ties provide stability

uk_ireland.historical_enmity: 800 years of conflict; Irish War of Independence; The Troubles
uk_ireland.transformation: Good Friday Agreement (1998); CTA (Common Travel Area)
uk_ireland.lesson: Creative constitutional arrangements can resolve territorial disputes

if_similar_transformation: Combined GDP could challenge China ($5T+ by 2040)
South Asia as global manufacturing hub
1.8 billion people benefit from peace dividend

historical_leaders.quote: There can be no lasting peace unless there is a change in hearts and minds.
historical_leaders.author: Mahatma Gandhi

historical_leaders.quote: We cannot change our history. But we can change our future.
historical_leaders.author: Atal Bihari Vajpayee

historical_leaders.quote: No two nations are as close as India and Pakistan. We share blood, culture, history.
historical_leaders.author: Benazir Bhutto

historical_leaders.quote: Peace is not absence of conflict, it is the ability to handle conflict by peaceful means.
historical_leaders.author: Ronald Reagan

historical_leaders.quote: We may have all come on different ships, but we're in the same boat now.
historical_leaders.author: Martin Luther King Jr.

vision_quote.quote: Imagine a day when a student in Lahore studies in Delhi, a patient in Karachi gets treatment in Mumbai, a businessman in Amritsar sells in Faisalabad, and families divided in 1947 finally reunite. This is not a dream - it is a choice.

voices.message: My grandmother's village is 30 km away in India. She died without seeing it again. Our grandchildren should not suffer the same fate.
We can see Lahore's lights from our rooftops. How can we be enemies?
We want peace. Not peace of the grave, but peace with dignity.
When India and Pakistan play cricket, my heart races. Imagine if that passion was for building together, not just competing.

possible_outcomes: Internal challenges: Federalism, communal tensions (but manageable with democratic framework)

scenarios.scenario: Partition but peaceful relations from start

possible_outcomes: Continued human suffering (divided families, conflict victims)

what_if_1947_was_different.lesson: History is shaped by choices. Future choices matter more than past grievances.

for_citizens: Support peace voices; reject hate speech
Engage with counterparts via social media (positively)
for_diaspora: Lobby host governments for engagement policy
for_media: Cover peace efforts, not just conflicts
for_governments: Take the first step; don't wait for preconditions

charts.title: Two Futures: Peace vs Status Quo

15_us_intervention_analysis.section_description: Analysis of US involvement in India-Pakistan dynamics, Afghanistan impact, alternative scenarios

overview.summary: US has been the most significant external actor in India-Pakistan relations, with interventions ranging from military alliances to crisis mediation to strategic partnerships
phases: Cold War (1947-1991): Alliance with Pakistan vs Non-aligned India
War on Terror (2001-2021): Pakistan as ally; India as partner
Indo-Pacific Era (2021-present): Decisive tilt toward India

frameworks.name: Mutual Defense Assistance Agreement
frameworks.content: US military aid and equipment to Pakistan

military_aid.platforms: F-86 Sabres, M47/M48 Patton tanks, artillery

impact_on_india: 1965 war: Pakistani Patton tanks vs Indian Centurions

1971_crisis.us_position: Supported Pakistan; sent USS Enterprise to Bay of Bengal
1971_crisis.india_response: USSR support; rapid victory; Bangladesh created
1971_crisis.long_term_impact: Deep Indian mistrust of US (lasted until 2000s)

u2_incident_pakistan.detail: US U-2 spy plane shot down over USSR flew from Peshawar, Pakistan
u2_incident_pakistan.significance: Pakistan's strategic value to US; India's concerns validated

us_pakistan_role: Pakistan granted access for US military operations
us_india_role: India offered bases; US declined (chose Pakistan)
India provided humanitarian aid to Afghanistan

turning_points.outcome: India restrained; Pakistan pressured on LeT

turning_points.us_action: Unilateral operation in Abbottabad without Pakistan knowledge
turning_points.india_reaction: Vindication of Pakistan terror safe haven claims

dynamics: India sidelined from Afghanistan peace process

pakistan_impact: Taliban return seen as strategic gain
phase_4_withdrawal.us_pakistan_current: Relations at lowest point; transactional only

lessons: US-Pakistan alliance was transactional; not values-based
India's patience rewarded as US pivoted away from Pakistan
Afghanistan intervention destabilized region; didn't resolve India-Pakistan tensions

crisis_list.us_role: Deputy National Security Advisor Robert Gates visited both capitals

us_role: Clinton refused to bail out Pakistan
crisis_list.significance: US sided with India against Pakistan ally

us_role: US pressured Pakistan on terror groups
crisis_list.source: US State Department; Indian government accounts

crisis_list.outcome: India did not launch military strike; diplomatic response

crisis_list.us_role: Obama administration urged restraint; acknowledged India's right to respond
crisis_list.outcome: Limited Indian surgical strikes; no escalation

us_role: US acknowledged India's right to self-defense
crisis_list.outcome: Limited aerial skirmish; de-escalation within days

pattern_observed: US intervened to prevent nuclear escalation
Post-2008: US increasingly sided with India
Crisis management effective but didn't address root causes

us_india_partnership.source: US State Department; White House fact sheets

us_pakistan_current.future: Unlikely to return to alliance; India more valuable partner

us_on_kashmir.official_position: Bilateral issue between India and Pakistan
evolution: 2019 (Article 370): US called for calm; did not criticize India
Current: Treats as internal Indian matter

trump_mediation_offer_2019.event: Trump offered to mediate on Kashmir (July 2019)
trump_mediation_offer_2019.outcome: US walked back; acknowledged India's position

what_if_us_not_allied_pakistan_cold_war.scenario: US maintained neutrality between India and Pakistan (1954 onwards)
possible_outcomes: Pakistan not militarily strengthened (no F-86s, Pattons)
1965 war might not have occurred or been less intense
India might not have tilted toward USSR
Nuclear programs might have developed differently

what_if_us_supported_kashmir_resolution_.scenario: US actively mediated Kashmir in 1950s with teeth
possible_outcomes: Either unified Kashmir in one country or clear division
Core dispute resolved before nuclear weapons
obstacles_then: Cold War priorities trumped regional stability
Pakistan alliance more valuable than India friendship

what_if_no_afghanistan_war.scenario: US did not invade Afghanistan post-9/11 (pursued alternatives)
possible_outcomes: India-US relationship might have developed faster
Regional instability might have been less
what_if_no_afghanistan_war.note: Highly speculative; 9/11 response was politically inevitable

what_if_sco_regional_framework.scenario: US supported SCO-led regional security architecture including India-Pakistan
description: Instead of bilateral US alliances, multilateral framework
China, Russia, India, Pakistan, Central Asia in same security structure
obstacles: US unwilling to share regional influence

regional_autonomy_scenario.concept: South Asia managing own affairs without external great power dominance

approach: India-Pakistan both engage US, China, Russia
No exclusive alliances that threaten neighbors

obstacles: US-China competition pulls countries into camps
Nuclear weapons make stakes too high for regional resolution
potential_catalysts: New leadership committed to regional vision
External shocks reducing great power interest

recommendations.recommendation: Encourage India-Pakistan dialogue without taking sides
recommendations.rationale: US credibility highest when seen as honest broker
recommendations.obstacles: India prefers no US role; Pakistan wants US pressure on India

recommendations.recommendation: Condition any Pakistan engagement on terror action
recommendations.rationale: Leverage limited remaining influence for regional stability
Government-to-government frozen; civil society can maintain links

recommendations.recommendation: Integrate South Asia into Indo-Pacific framework inclusively
recommendations.rationale: Currently India-centric; broader engagement could help

us_policy_recommendations.source: Various think-tank reports: Carnegie, CSIS, Brookings, RAND

negatives: Cold War alliance fueled arms race
assessment_summary.current_trajectory: US firmly in India's camp; Pakistan drifting toward China; bilateral resolution less likely without US balanced engagement

charts.title: US Crisis Interventions in India-Pakistan Conflicts
US Alliance Shift: Pakistan to India

16_metadata_sources.section_description: Data quality information, source citations, methodology, update schedule

historical_events.sources: Government archives, UN records, academic consensus

current_statistics.sources: Government reports (2023-2024), IMF, World Bank, SIPRI
current_statistics.note: Some figures rounded or estimated where exact data unavailable

military_data.sources: SIPRI, IISS Military Balance, government statements
military_data.note: Nuclear arsenal sizes are estimates; classified data unavailable

peace_scenario_quantification.sources: World Bank studies, ICRIER, academic estimates
peace_scenario_quantification.note: Indicative figures based on modeling; actual outcomes would vary

data_gaps: Informal trade exact volumes (by nature unmeasured)

conflicting_data_handling.principle: Present both positions when governments differ
examples: Balakot casualties: India claims significant; Pakistan denies any
2019 aerial engagement: Both claim shooting down opponent aircraft
Kashmir: Both claim entire territory; presented as positions not facts

international_organizations.name: World Bank
international_organizations.key_reports: World Development Indicators; Doing Business; Trade reports

international_organizations.name: International Monetary Fund (IMF)
international_organizations.key_databases: World Economic Outlook; Direction of Trade Statistics

international_organizations.name: Stockholm International Peace Research Institute (SIPRI)
international_organizations.key_publications: SIPRI Yearbook; Arms Transfers Database; Military Expenditure Database

academic_research.relevant_work: South Asia program; nuclear security research

academic_research.institution: Center for Strategic and International Studies (CSIS)
Institute of Strategic Studies Islamabad (ISSI) - Pakistan

academic_research.relevant_work: Nuclear risk reduction; South Asia security

news_verification.note: Primary international wire service for fact verification

data_collection: News sources for recent events (verified against official statements)
conflict_resolution: When India-Pakistan positions differ, both presented with sources
International/neutral sources used as reference point
No editorial judgment on disputed claims
quantification_approach: SIPRI, World Bank, IMF for standardized international data
